title: 223 By BP
description: This dataset comprises laser-evoked pain responses from participants who received fixed-intensity nociceptive stimuli at varying energy levels (low pain: 3J/3.5J; high pain: 3.5J/4J) while providing subjective pain intensity ratings on a 0-10 scale (0=no sensation, 4=pain onset, 6=moderate pain, 8=severe pain, 10=unbearable pain). The experiment involved 30 laser stimuli delivered across 3 blocks with balanced distribution of pain intensities. This dataset supports investigation of the neural correlates of pain perception and subjective pain intensity assessment.
methods_description: Participants received laser stimulation at fixed intensities (low pain: 3J/3.5J; high pain: 3.5J/4J) in 3 blocks of 10 stimuli each, with high and low pain stimuli evenly distributed within each block. Following each stimulus, a 5-second pause preceded subjective pain intensity ratings on a 0-10 scale (0=no sensation, 4=pain onset, 6=moderate pain, 8=severe pain, 10=unbearable pain). Participants were instructed to maintain visual fixation on a crosshair display and keep eyes open throughout stimulation. Subsequent trials began at random intervals of 5 seconds after rating completion.
